tldr: The swing trading system identified 10 bullish setups across large-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, META, GOOGL, AMZN, AMD) and select financials/consumer (JPM), plus 2 shorts (NKE, XOM) on strong downtrends, with 2 holds (TSLA, GS) due to conflicting signals. The portfolio targets 5-14 day holds on positions sized for 2-4:1 risk-reward, emphasizing trend-following and momentum strategies while managing extreme overbought conditions through position sizing.
narrative:
Over the past year, the market has entered a pronounced tech rally, with semiconductor and cloud leaders accelerating while traditional energy and consumer segments lag. This swing analysis ran 9 strategy agents (trend-followers, momentum rankers, breakout hunters, mean-reversion traders, and more) on 14 major tickers, synthesized by the Head Swing Trader.

The core finding: multi-timeframe alignment (daily EMAs in bullish order, hourly uptrends confirmed, ADX above 35) is the strongest signal. Seven tickers show textbook bullish setups—MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMD, META, AMZN, and SPY/QQQ indices—with trend followers and momentum rankers in agreement (72-85% confidence). The technical consensus is overwhelming: these markets are in structural uptrends with accelerating rates-of-change. Stanley Druckenmiller's fundamental lens (which weighs revenue and earnings growth) agrees on NVDA and AMD but questions META, GOOGL, and AMZN on negative or slowing earnings.

A critical tension runs through the analysis: extreme overbought conditions (RSI 82-91, Z-scores 1.7-3.7) sit alongside strong momentum. Mean Reversion agents flag pullback risk, but the Head Trader sized positions modestly (AMD 15 shares despite 83% agent conviction; SPY/QQQ trimmed to avoid double-counting tech exposure with single-name longs) to harvest upside while respecting drawdown limits. AMD's 41.7% 21-day ROC and hourly STC momentum divergence exemplify this: confidence is high, but RSI 91 and an hourly STC drop below 75 suggest caution—so position size was cut in half.

On the short side, consensus is clearer. NKE faces a -17% 21-day downtrend with collapsing EPS (-8.8%), and XOM shows negative momentum across all timeframes with -10.3% EPS. Both trades enforce tight 2:1 risk-reward minimums to hedge against oversold bounces (Mean Reversion noted XOM and NKE as technically oversold, but the absence of bullish reversal candles kept the short lean intact).

Two holds stand out for clarity: TSLA's multi-timeframe conflict (daily downtrend vs. hourly uptrend, 5d ROC +12.5% vs. 21d ROC -2.6%, RSI bouncing between 56 and trending lower) makes it untradeble until one timeframe takes control. Similarly, GS weakened on momentum deceleration and weak daily ADX (20.66), making it not worth the risk.

The final portfolio spreads 10 longs, 2 shorts, and 2 holds across 14 tickers, targeting 5-14 day holds at 2-4:1 risk-reward ratios. Sector concentration (tech longs inter-correlated 0.67-0.96) was actively managed: large positions in NVDA (50 shares), GOOGL (45), and AMZN (35) are offset by much smaller SPY (15) and QQQ (12) to avoid redundancy. XOM's -0.04 correlation to the book serves as a genuine hedge.

AAPL.verdict: Buy 40 shares at 262.50 (60% confidence, 5-8 trading days).
AAPL.bull_case: Daily and hourly trends aligned; Trend Follower and Pullback Trader both bullish; Momentum Ranker shows 62% confidence. Sector Rotation bullish on tech inflows. Breakout Trader sees clean technical setup.
AAPL.bear_case: Valuation weak (31.4x P/E) with flat revenue growth. Mean Reversion flags RSI 62 approaching overbought. Druckenmiller bearish on fundamentals. Momentum is technical-driven, not event-driven (Catalyst Trader neutral).

key_numbers.rsi: 62 (moderate, not yet overbought; above 70 is exhaustion)
key_numbers.pe_ratio: 31.4x (expensive vs. market avg ~20x, but justified in growth leaders)

AAPL.risk_summary: Fundamental overhang from weak earnings growth caps upside. If technical breakdown occurs (close below 256.50), stop-loss triggers. Mean Reversion argues for a pullback within the uptrend. Position sized modestly (40 shares) to reflect 60% conviction.

MSFT.bear_case: RSI 82 signals overbought exhaustion. Pullback Trader bearish (35% confidence). Mean Reversion bearish (72% confidence) on Z-score 1.79 overbought. A pullback is likely within trend. Druckenmiller neutral on balanced risk-reward.

MSFT.risk_summary: Overbought technicals (RSI 82, STC 98.9) mean a pullback to 412 could occur before the 442 target. Entry discipline required; size cut to 25 shares to reflect overbought caution despite 72% conviction.

TSLA.bull_case: Hourly uptrend (398 > 391 > 377); Trend Follower 72% bullish on hourly pullback setup. Breakout Trader 68% bullish on 113M share volume 4/15. Short-term 5d ROC +12.5% shows momentum.
TSLA.bear_case: Daily downtrend (368 < 371 < 387) contradicts hourly uptrend—multi-timeframe conflict. 21d ROC -2.6% vs. 5d ROC +12.5% means momentum divergence (5-day bounce within larger downtrend). Valuation egregious (212x P/E, 420x P/FCF). Druckenmiller bearish. Momentum Ranker neutral (45%).

key_numbers.multi_timeframe_conflict: Daily downtrend vs. hourly uptrend; EMAs misaligned daily

TSLA.risk_summary: Conflicting timeframes prevent a high-conviction trade. If daily breaks above 387, hourly uptrend could take control and justify a buy. If hourly breaks below 377, daily downtrend reasserts. R:R only 1.64:1 falls below system's 2.0:1 minimum. Stand aside until one timeframe wins.

Sized tiny (12 shares, ~$7,700) to avoid double-counting exposure—this is a portfolio hedge to tech rotation, not a primary position. Stop at 634 is tight.

key_themes: Tech rally with extreme overbought technicals: RSI 82-91, Z-scores 1.7-3.7 signal pullbacks within uptrends—sized positions modestly to capture upside while respecting mean reversion
Multi-timeframe alignment wins: Daily + hourly EMAs in bullish order with ADX 35+ (trend strong) = highest-conviction setups (GOOGL 82%, NVDA 78%, AMD 83%)

Fundamental-technical split: Tech longs (NVDA, AMD) show structural earnings growth (19-39% EPS) despite overbought, while others (META, GOOGL, AMZN) rely on margin expansion with earnings weakness—Druckenmiller caution noted but overruled by trend strength in short-term swing window

Shorts on structural decline: NKE (-17% 21d, -8.8% EPS) and XOM (-10.3% EPS, sector outflow) are downtrends with fundamental deterioration—mean reversion bounces expected but within downtrend structure

Correlation management: Tech long book (0.67-0.96 inter-correlation) concentrated in NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN (50, 45, 35 shares); SPY/QQQ index positions reduced (15/12 shares) to avoid double-counting; XOM short (-0.04 correlation) = genuine portfolio hedge
Risk-reward enforcement: All trades target R:R ≥ 2.0:1 per mandate—tight stops adjusted or targets extended to meet minimum; TSLA/GS holds on low R:R and conflicting signals

Position sizing discipline: Overbought severity inversely proportional to size (AMD 15 shares despite 83% conviction due to RSI 91 + Z-score 3.66; SPY/QQQ 15/12 shares to avoid tech double-count; JPM 30 modest due to 62% and deteriorating momentum)

glossary.EMA: Exponential Moving Average—a trend line that reacts quickly to recent price moves. EMAs in bullish order (short > mid > long) mean the stock is trending up; downtrend order (short < mid < long) means down.
glossary.ADX: Average Directional Index (0-100)—measures trend strength, not direction. Below 25 = no trend (choppy); 25-40 = moderate trend; above 40 = strong trend. Helps confirm whether EMAs are actually controlling price.
glossary.RSI: Relative Strength Index (0-100)—measures momentum. Below 30 = oversold (bounce possible); above 70 = overbought (pullback likely); 30-70 = balanced. RSI 82-91 is extreme exhaustion.
glossary.Z-Score: Number of standard deviations away from the 50-day moving average. 0 = at average; 1 = 1 standard deviation above (moderately high); 2+ = extreme (pullback likely). High Z-scores signal mean reversion, not continuation.
glossary.ROC: Rate of Change—the % gain/loss over a timeframe (5d, 10d, 21d). Positive ROC = uptrend; negative ROC = downtrend. Accelerating ROC (5d > 10d > 21d) signals strengthening momentum.
glossary.Fibonacci Support/Resistance: Price levels (50%, 38.2%, 23.6% retracements) where bounces often occur in pullbacks. Used to set stops and targets in trends.
glossary.Sector Rotation: Money flowing from one sector to another (e.g., tech outperforming energy). Measured by relative strength and volume. Indicates institutional conviction.
glossary.Overbought/Oversold: When a stock's technicals (RSI, Stochastic, Z-score) suggest it has moved too far up or down too fast, making a pullback likely (not guaranteed). Overbought stocks often bounce down; oversold stocks bounce up.
glossary.Risk-Reward Ratio: The amount risked (from entry to stop-loss) vs. amount gained (from entry to target). A 3:1 ratio means risking $1 to make $3—favorable odds. Trades with <2:1 R:R are typically rejected.
glossary.Breakout: When price breaks above a prior resistance level (e.g., previous high) on strong volume. Suggests institutional buying and potential for continued uptrend.
glossary.Pullback: A temporary reversal within a larger trend (e.g., 5-10% downmove in an uptrend). Used by traders to enter at better prices within uptrends.
glossary.STC: Schaff Trend Cycle—an oscillator (0-100) that detects overbought (>75) and oversold (<25) conditions. Useful on intraday timeframes. Crossing below 75 in an uptrend signals momentum divergence (fade).
glossary.Momentum Divergence: When price makes a new high but momentum (ROC, RSI, STC) fails to confirm—signals momentum fading and reversal risk.
glossary.Volume: Number of shares traded. 1.5x average volume on breakout = institutional buying (bullish); 0.6x volume on downtrend = weak selling (vulnerable to bounce).
glossary.Correlation: How two assets move together. +0.9 = move in lockstep (redundant if you own both); 0 = independent; -0.1 = uncorrelated (good for hedging portfolio risk).
glossary.Holding Period: Expected duration the system expects to hold the trade (5-14 trading days in swing mode). Longer-dated targets allow for pullbacks and second entries.
glossary.Timeframe: Time horizon of analysis (daily = 24H bars; hourly = 1H bars; 5-minute = intraday candles). Multi-timeframe analysis (daily + hourly) provides confirmation and detects conflicts.
glossary.Druckenmiller: Stanley Druckenmiller—legendary macro trader in this system; focuses on earnings growth, revenue, and macro momentum. Often bearish on valuations and momentum divergence.
glossary.Head Swing Trader: The synthesizing agent that reads all 9 strategy signals and makes final buy/hold/short decisions with position sizing.
